t_ch: 什么文件？我也排队！
t_en: What documents? I'm getting in line too!

t_ch: 我看你什么都不懂，只知道在这里瞎凑热闹。
t_en: You don't understand anything, do you? You just came here to rubberneck.

t_ch: 我没有等天舶司的文件。
t_en: I'm not waiting for documents from the Sky-Faring Commission.

t_ch: 嗐嗐嗐，你早说啊，非得等我批评你几句，你才说清楚。
t_en: Hey hey, why didn't you say so? Did you have to wait for me to say all that before you made things clear?

t_ch: 既然你不是来这里等文件的化外民商人，那我们俩也不是同行冤家了。
t_en: Since you're not an Outworlder merchant waiting here for documents, then I suppose we aren't rivals.

t_ch: 反倒是那个麦尔彦，嚯嚯嚯！什么东西，跟她呼吸同一片空气都让人觉得辱没了自己。
t_en: On the contrary, that Maryam — hohoho! What a wreck, it's a disgrace just to be within spitting distance of her.

t_ch: 要是按照我们「埃伊加文明」家乡的习惯，我本应该向她发起「决斗」，用拳头来彰显自身尊严。可惜这里是仙舟，你懂的，讲文明讲礼貌不能动拳脚……
t_en: If we followed the Aigaian Customs of my homeworld, I would have challenged her to a "duel" and fought for dignity with my fists. A shame we're on the Xianzhou. Know what I'm saying? All uppity and civilized, can't get into a scrap here...

t_ch: 我听说还是有人在仙舟决斗的。
t_en: I've heard that people still duel on the Xianzhou.

t_ch: 你不会也看什么《守护星槎海》吧？那节目只是一知半解，没讲对。
t_en: Let me guess, you're a fan of Protecting Starskiff Haven, right? That program is so superficial. They get it all wrong.

t_ch: 再说了，我每次想发起「决斗」时，那个大尾巴的商会专员就会说「不能打架！不能打架！商会批文机会少，谁有耐心就给他」。
t_en: Besides, every time I want to "duel," that guild rep with a big tail will always say, "No fighting! No fighting! Guild approval is a rare privilege reserved for the patient!"

t_ch: 为了这些文件，我只好忍住。
t_en: For the sake of these documents, I had to hold back.

t_ch: 你也知道这不文明啊。
t_en: You know it's disorderly conduct.

t_ch: 入乡随俗嘛，换作我的家乡，用「决斗」解决分歧才是正确的礼仪。
t_en: Gotta follow the local customs, right? Back in my hometown, it's proper etiquette to settle disputes with a "duel."

t_ch: 还有那令人吃惊的娱乐商品年龄分级制度…我那位在公司娱乐部门的朋友成天为这儿「<unbreak>200</unbreak>岁以下不可观看」的分级制度感到头疼。
t_en: As for those astonishing age restrictions for entertainment... My friend who works in the Entertainment Division keeps telling me how much of a problem the "Restricted for those under <unbreak>200</unbreak> years old" rating system is.

t_ch: 要知道在咱们埃伊加，十二岁就能持有私人武装了。
t_en: Back on Aigaia, personal weaponry is legislated for ages twelve and up.

t_ch: 你们俩之间有什么不对付吗？
t_en: Have you two come to a disagreement?

t_ch: 您知道赛伯邑超导合金吗？我手上屯了不少，准备来仙舟赚票大的，但是没想到那个麦尔彦也有相同的打算，她打算抢我的生意。
t_en: Ever heard of Cybercity superconducting alloys? I had a whole stockpile and was ready to come to the Xianzhou and make it big... Until I found out about Maryam having the same plan and wanting to steal all of my business.

t_ch: 我不服气呀，我宁愿亏钱，也决不能让天舶司的商团采购那家伙的合金。
t_en: I can't accept it. I'd rather lose money than let the Sky-Faring Commission buy her stock.

t_ch: …我这人说话有些冲，老家的朋友说我要是不改，以后做不了生意。刚才我要是多有冒犯，请您不要介意。
t_en: ...I know I come off as rather aggressive, and my friends back home always remind me that this'll run me out of business one day. If I've offended you just now, please don't take it to heart.

t_ch: 这份见面礼你收下吧，以后要是有机会，咱们还得做生意呐。对了，你现在有空吗，要不咱们再聊一会？
t_en: Take this gift for meeting up with me. If we get a chance to work together in the future, then let's do some business. Do you want to chat more?

t_ch: 想跟你打听一些事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you about something.

t_ch: 尽管打听，我要是能回答，我会告诉你的。
t_en: Ask away. If I'm able to answer, I'll be sure to let you know.

t_ch: 能讲讲「埃伊加文明」吗？
t_en: What's this "Aigaian" you speak of?

t_ch: 怎么？你也对我们埃伊加文明感兴趣？也好，容我给你好好讲讲，也省得你再产生什么刻板印象。
t_en: Huh? Are you interested in our Aigaian civilization too? That's great. Let me fill you in so you don't formulate any negative stereotypes.

t_ch: 我们埃伊加文明，几千年前是一群各自为政的星际牧民，在「领航天蛾」的导向下，在许多行星之间逐水草而居。很多时候，也会为了些资源兄弟阋墙。
t_en: Thousands of years ago, our Aigaian civilization was just a group of interstellar herders who did things our own way. Under the guidance of the Navigator Moth, we'd live a nomadic lifestyle across several planets. In many cases, internecine quarrels would break out over resources.

t_ch: 直到后来，「埃伊加汗国」的雄主普朗克•埃伊加可汗觉得，大家同文同种，也都信仰「存护」，何不统一成一个强大的联盟呢？
t_en: Until one day, the almighty Planck Aigaia Khan, hero of the Aigaian Khanate, realized an ambition — with everyone utilizing the same language, culture, and all worshiping the Preservation, why not unite under a powerful alliance together?

t_ch: 最终，在普朗克汗的文治武功之下，「埃伊加汗国」将「古登堡汗国」、「黎曼汗国」和「海森堡汗国」归于一统，成为了现在的「埃伊加文明」。
t_en: In the end, Planck Khan's cultural and military achievements unified the Aigaia, Gutenberg, Riemann, and Heisenberg Khanates under one banner — the almighty Aigaian civilization, which we know to this day.

t_ch: 再后来，星际和平公司与我们建立了联系，「埃伊加文明」才初次加入到信用点结算体系之中，这不，我才能来到仙舟做生意嘛。
t_en: Later on, the IPC made the first contact with us, and the Aigaian civilization was officially added to the credits macro-economy. Only then could I finally come to the Xianzhou to conduct business affairs.

t_ch: 我们现在「决斗」的传统，就是来自普朗克可汗。他觉得人类的尊严就应该在一个人与另一个人公平的「决斗」中彰显。
t_en: Our modern tradition of "dueling" comes from Planck Khan. He felt that human dignity should be demonstrated via a fair "duel" between two individuals.

t_ch: 所以你明白了吗？「决斗」可不是什么「野蛮的习俗」！而是我们彰显尊严的方式！
t_en: So do you get it now? Dueling isn't a barbaric custom at all! Rather, it's our display of human dignity!

t_ch: 你手上的这批货要怎么办？
t_en: What are you going to do with your batch of goods?

t_ch: 能怎么办呢？总不能烂在我手里了吧…哎，我不是说赛伯邑超导合金会烂掉，我是说自己不能什么都没赚到就回老家了。
t_en: What can I do? I can't just leave them to rot... *sigh* I don't mean that Cybercity superconducting alloys could ever actually rot. I just mean that I can't go back to my hometown with my tail between my legs and nothing to show for it.

t_ch: 我老家的贸易不发达，自从星际和平公司入驻以后，继续做本地生意也到头了，所以我和家乡的几个小伙伴一合计，凑了钱，搭了伙找找天外的机会。
t_en: Ours isn't a hyper-developed star system. Once the IPC settled in, that was the end of our local business economy. So, I teamed up with a few hometown friends, pooling our money to make opportunities for ourselves beyond the sky.

t_ch: 结果到最后只有我一人来到了这仙舟罗浮，这也不打紧，只要我能把生意谈成了，家乡那几位也不会觉得血本无归。
t_en: I ended up being the only one who made it to the Xianzhou Luofu, which was no big deal. People back home wouldn't feel like they were losing their shirts as long as I can get some business going for us.

t_ch: 我听说工造司本来是需要我手上这些超导合金的，需求量不大，结果半路杀出个麦尔彦，要拿下批文，还得跟她竞价…我非得急死。
t_en: I heard they needed these superconducting alloys over at the Artisanship Commission, but not very much. But Maryam popped out of nowhere and wants the business for herself. Now I have to compete with her prices... I'm so anxious!

t_ch: 她降价，我也降价，我总得卖出去，不能让她占了便宜。
t_en: She cut her price, then so did I. I had to make this sale and couldn't let her seize the advantage from me.

t_ch: 去天舶司里打听打听如何？
t_en: What about making inquiries over at the Sky-Faring Commission?

t_ch: 嗐嗐嗐，你以为我没去过吗？我去过好几遍了。
t_en: C'mon... Do you really think I haven't tried? I've been there many times already.

t_ch: 天舶司的态度是挺好的，怪就怪我遇上了建木生发，关闭玉界门，暂停商团批文，天舶司早就忙得焦头烂额了，我也能理解他们有多辛苦。
t_en: The Sky-Faring Commission has been reasonable. I can only blame the Ambrosial Arbor's new growth, the closing of the Jade Gate, and the cessation of trade permits. They have been burned out for a while over at the Sky-Faring Commission and I can understand how hard they've been working.

t_ch: 灾难发生之后我就悟了，世界上从来就没有什么「稳赚不赔的生意」，按照仙舟的俗语来说就是「时也，运也，命也」。
t_en: After the disaster, I came to realize that there's no such thing as a steady and stable business out there in the universe. Or as a common Xianzhou saying puts it, "It's a matter of time, luck, and fate."

t_ch: 这位叫茂贞的小哥愿意帮我解决问题，我也相信他能帮我处理批文…我就待在这里，关键是那个麦尔彦…要是能识相点就好了。
t_en: There's a guy called Maozhen who's keen to help me solve things. I trust he can help me with document approval... So I'm staying here. The key is Maryam... If she could just be a little more sensible.

t_ch: 有机会下次再聊吧。
t_en: Catch you again next time.

t_ch: 行啊，在外面做生意，咱们还得多聊。
t_en: Cool. Outworld traders like us need to stick together.

t_ch: 有机会下次再聊吧。
t_en: Catch you again next time.

t_ch: 行啊，在外面做生意，咱们还得多聊。
t_en: Cool. Outworld traders like us need to stick together.

t_ch: 这次的商会批文我是志在必得！
t_en: This time, I'm determined to win that guild permit!

t_ch: 那个麦尔彦真不傻！要是批文交给她，我就没法回到家，绝不能够交给她！
t_en: That Maryam sure is sharp! If she obtains approval, then I won't be able to go home, so she absolutely must not get it!

t_ch: 你来了，我还没拿到商会批文呢，你现在有空吗，要不咱们再聊一会？
t_en: You're back. I haven't been approved yet. Are you free now for a quick chat?

t_ch: 想跟你打听一些事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you about something.

t_ch: 尽管打听，我要是能回答，我会告诉你的。
t_en: Ask away. If I'm able to answer, I'll be sure to let you know.

t_en: What's this "Aigaian" you speak of?

t_en: She cut her price, then so did I. I had to make this sale and couldn't let her seize the advantage from me.

t_en: What about making inquiries over at the Sky-Faring Commission?

t_ch: 嗐嗐嗐，你以为我没去过吗？我去过好几遍了。
t_en: C'mon... Do you really think I haven't tried? I've been there many times already.

t_ch: 天舶司的态度是挺好的，怪就怪我遇上了建木生发，关闭玉界门，暂停商团批文，天舶司早就忙得焦头烂额了，我也能理解他们有多辛苦。

t_ch: 这位叫茂贞的小哥愿意帮我解决问题，我也相信他能帮我处理批文…我就待在这里，关键是那个麦尔彦…要是能识相点就好了。
t_en: There's a guy called Maozhen who's keen to help me solve things. I trust he can help me with document approval... So I'm staying here. The key is Maryam... If she could just be a little more sensible.

t_ch: 有机会下次再聊吧。
t_en: Catch you again next time.

t_ch: 行啊，在外面做生意，咱们还得多聊。
t_en: Cool. Outworld traders like us need to stick together.

t_ch: 有机会下次再聊吧。
t_en: Catch you again next time.

t_ch: 行啊，在外面做生意，咱们还得多聊。
t_en: Cool. Outworld traders like us need to stick together.

t_ch: 天舶司的条例好像又更新了一版，主要修订了因建木生发的相关滞留和赔偿内容，看起来没有和商业批文有关的内容……
t_en: Seems like the Sky-Faring Commission has updated their regulations yet again, mostly about detentions and compensation due to the Ambrosial Arbor's new growth. But there doesn't seem to be anything about document approvals...

t_ch: 老爷你好，你也是来自仙舟外的行商吗？我叫麦尔彦，主要从事的是赛伯邑超导合金的分发和销售，如果老爷感兴趣的话，我们可以聊聊。
t_en: Hello there boss, are you also a traveling merchant from beyond the sky? My name is Maryam, and I'm mostly involved with the distribution and sales of Cybercity superconducting alloys. Let's talk if you're interested.

t_ch: 我不是域外行商，不做生意。
t_en: I'm no Outworld traveling merchant, and I'm not into business.

t_ch: 不是域外行商，那没有关系，我还担心这里又来一位卖赛伯邑超导合金的主。
t_en: Doesn't matter whether you're an Outworld traveling merchant or not. I'm more concerned about another vendor trying to sell Cybercity superconducting alloys out here.

t_ch: 仙舟上说「同行是冤家」，又一说「冤家路窄」，这就说明「同行路走窄了」。咱俩不是同行，这路肯定是越走越宽阔。
t_en: On the Xianzhou, there's a saying: "Your peers are your rivals" — and another one: "Rivals will always run into each other," meaning rivals tread a narrow path alongside each other. We are no rivals, so the road we take should surely widen out.

t_ch: 那边那位叫辛克莱的行商就不一定了。无论你跟他是不是同行，我建议你少跟他打交道，会折寿的，咱们都是短生种还得珍惜寿命。
t_en: But not necessarily for that traveling merchant over there called Sinclair. I suggest you keep away from him whether you're our rival or not for longevity's sake. We're all short-life species here, so life is to be cherished.

t_ch: 行商为什么会在流云渡里？
t_en: Why would a traveling merchant come to Cloudford?

t_ch: 老爷你这问法，确实是一个外行人。
t_en: You really do sound like an outsider when you ask questions like that, boss.

t_ch: 我带了一批货物停放在流云渡的积玉坊里，虽然天舶司承诺了货物安全，但是这附近总归是有些孽物不太安全，我经常来这里清点货物。
t_en: I loaded up a bunch of my stock into the Trove of Verdure here in Cloudford. Despite the Sky-Faring Commission guaranteeing security for me, there's always an abomination or two nearby, so I often stop in to check on my stock.

t_ch: 另外，我感觉…只是我感觉啊，仙舟人对时间的感受有一些问题。
t_en: Also, I just feel... It's just a feeling I've got that Xianzhou people have an unusual conception of time.

t_ch: 对他们来说「十年不算长」，就好像是「要不了多久」的程度，你要是不催催他们，在他们的观念里这件事就不太着急。
t_en: For them, ten years is like a snap of the fingers, so when they tell you things like "soon," you have to keep on pressing them because in their minds, it's not an urgent matter.

t_ch: 这件事不能等，所以老爷你看，那位叫作茂贞的老哥就被我天天追问。
t_en: This matter can't wait though, so you will see me chasing up that guy named Maozhen every single day.

t_ch: 我虽然杵在这里，能做的也只是一边查看天舶司的条例，一边焦急地等待，要是老爷你愿意跟我随便聊聊，也算是一种打发时间的方法，如何？
t_en: So I'm stuck here. All I can do is check up on the Sky-Faring Commission's regulations and pace around anxiously. If you're down for a casual chat with me, it'd be a great way to pass the time — how about it?

t_ch: 想要随便聊聊天。
t_en: Let's have a small chat.

t_ch: 求之不得，老爷你想聊些什么呢？
t_en: That's all I could ask for. What would you like to chat about?

t_ch: 看上去你和那位辛克莱不对付？
t_en: I'm guessing you and that Sinclair guy don't get along?

t_ch: 其实吧，要我说，我从来没有把他当成一回事。
t_en: Honestly, if you ask me, I've never taken him seriously.

t_ch: 那位辛克莱老爷总觉得我要害他，我是怀着恶意来抢他的生意的。我提供一个报价，他就把价格压低，我再提一个，他再压低……
t_en: Master Sinclair always thinks I'm out to get him, and that I'm this evil mastermind stealing his business. When I offer a price, he lowers it, and so I put out another price. And he lowers it again...

t_ch: 我怀疑他是来做慈善的，不是做生意的。一开始我也不想介意，后来想了想，我在外行商这么多年，怎么能在这种土老帽面前示弱。
t_en: I suspect he's here for charity instead of business. I didn't mind at first, but after thinking it through, as an experienced traveling merchant, how could I show any weakness in front of this oaf.

t_ch: 所以他想压价钱，我也奉陪。我还会比他更早拿到天舶司的批文，让他看看正经的行商都是怎么做的。
t_en: So if he wants to lower the price, I shall accompany him down. I'll get my documents from the Sky-Faring Commission approved before he does, too. That'll show him how a serious merchant gets business done.

t_ch: 你认识一位带着棺木的行商吗？
t_en: Do you know of a traveling merchant with a coffin?

t_ch: 带着棺木的行商？我没有见过…我也不认识所有的行商，要是我知道那位辛克莱也卖超导合金，我一开始就不会做这门生意，太晦气了。
t_en: A traveling merchant with a coffin? Not that I've seen... But then again, I don't know all the traveling merchants here. If I knew that Sinclair was already selling superconducting alloys, I wouldn't even have gotten into this trade. What rotten luck.

t_ch: 不过「带着棺木」听着就很神秘，我以前看过星际和平影视里的一个作品——孤独的枪手总是拖着副棺材漫步在泥泞的荒野上，从匪帮手里救下了一位姑娘。
t_en: But this "merchant with a coffin" you're asking about sounds very mysterious. I once saw a show on IPM with this solo gunslinger dragging along a coffin as he stalks the muddy wilderness, saving a girl from bandits' clutches.

t_ch: 枪手有着不幸的过去，脸上总是露出被背上回忆笼罩的表情。我一度以为棺材可能是空的——每当别人问他棺材里放着什么，他总是回答是自己。
t_en: The gunslinger had this troubled past that's etched onto a face clouded by memories. At one point I thought the coffin would be empty — every time someone asked him what was in the coffin, he replied that it was himself.

t_ch: 接着，我猜那里面是挚友或者亲人，是一种「不幸的象征」，他必须怀着愧疚心拖着棺木负重前进，但是无人提及棺中是否有腐蚀气味。
t_en: Then I guessed that it was a close friend or family member. A "symbol of misfortune" that forced him to drag the coffin out of guilt. But nobody mentioned any rotting stench emanating from within.

t_ch: 后来，我以为里面装着什么金银财宝，棺材只是一种掩人耳目的方式，你可能听说过银河里有一些不能被自然光直射的宝贝……
t_en: Later, I thought there must be a treasure or fortune inside, and the coffin was just a red herring. You may have heard that some cosmic treasures don't fare well under natural light...

t_ch: 结果枪手从棺木里掏出了一挺连射型重型能量机枪！哈哈哈哈哈，真是太令人意外了，仔细想想，他就是一名枪手，棺木里还能是什么呢？
t_en: So in the end, right, what does the gunslinger pull out of the coffin? A fully-automatic heavy energy machine gun! Ahahaha, what a surprise. Think about it! He's a gunslinger, so what else could be in the coffin?

t_ch: 所以你提到的那位「带着棺木的行商」既是行商，那棺木里多半就是商品了。
t_en: So if the "traveling merchant with a coffin" you mentioned is a traveling merchant, there should be goods for sale stuffed inside the coffin.

t_ch: 听上去你等公文很有经验？
t_en: You sound really experienced when it comes to waiting for official documents.

t_ch: 这…一言难尽啊。
t_en: It's... complicated.

t_ch: 我在家乡的时候，获取经商营业许可就已经够繁琐了，经常一等就是一年，一开始我还会有些愤怒还有焦虑，可是后来我也心态平和了。
t_en: When I was back on my home planet, getting a business license was always a real pain. It usually took a year. At first, I was pretty angry and anxious. But later on, I became more calm about it.

t_ch: 最后等到公文发下来的时候，我并没有那种很喜悦的感觉，就是麻木了，因为等得太久，我甚至都没能热烈地庆祝一番。
t_en: In the end, when the official document was finally issued, I didn't feel the kind of joy you'd expect. I was just so numb from waiting so long that I couldn't even get hyped up to celebrate.

t_ch: 麻烦总是会有的，「没有公文」不会是最难的那个。再说了，只要没人故意使坏，公文始终还是会下来的…好在，我们运气很好最后等到了。
t_en: There'll always be things that are a pain, and "Not having official documents" won't be the worst. I mean, as long as nobody deliberately messes things up, it'll get done sooner or later... Fortunately our luck held out until we got there in the end.

t_ch: 哎，我没有办法将这种事情叫作「经验」，请老爷见谅呀。
t_en: Hey, but I wouldn't call this any kind of "experience." Please forgive me.

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 慢走啊。
t_en: Off you go. Take care.

t_ch: 我还有事，先走了。
t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 慢走啊。
t_en: Off you go. Take care.

t_ch: 这次我才是第一个提交申请的，那个辛克莱老爷，最好再掂量掂量自己的分量。
t_en: This time my application was submitted first. And Master Sinclair should really reconsider his position.

t_ch: 哟，老爷你又来了？要再随便聊聊吗，反正闲着也是闲着。
t_en: Oh, you're still here, boss? Wanna have another chat? I'm not up to anything anyway.

t_ch: 想要随便聊聊天。
t_en: Let's have a small chat.

t_ch: 求之不得，老爷你想聊些什么呢？
t_en: That's all I could ask for. What would you like to chat about?

t_ch: 看上去你和那位辛克莱不对付？
t_en: I'm guessing you and that Sinclair guy don't get along?

t_ch: 其实吧，要我说，我从来没有把他当成一回事。
t_en: Honestly, if you ask me, I've never taken him seriously.

t_ch: 那位辛克莱老爷总觉得我要害他，我是怀着恶意来抢他的生意的。我提供一个报价，他就把价格压低，我再提一个，他再压低……
t_en: Master Sinclair always thinks I'm out to get him, and that I'm this evil mastermind stealing his business. When I offer a price, he lowers it, and so I put out another price. And he lowers it again...

t_ch: 我怀疑他是来做慈善的，不是做生意的。一开始我也不想介意，后来想了想，我在外行商这么多年，怎么能在这种土老帽面前示弱。
t_en: I suspect he's here for charity instead of business. I didn't mind at first, but after thinking it through, as an experienced traveling merchant, how could I show any weakness in front of this oaf.

t_ch: 所以他想压价钱，我也奉陪。我还会比他更早拿到天舶司的批文，让他看看正经的行商都是怎么做的。
t_en: So if he wants to lower the price, I shall accompany him down. I'll get my documents from the Sky-Faring Commission approved before he does, too. That'll show him how a serious merchant gets business done.

t_en: I'm busy, so I'll be heading off.

t_ch: 慢走啊。
t_en: Off you go. Take care.

t_ch: 又见面了，{NICKNAME}大人！您还是一如既往地潇洒帅气啊！
t_en: We meet again, esteemed {NICKNAME}! You are looking as dashing and charismatic as ever!

t_ch: 我们之前见过吗？
t_en: Have we met before?

t_ch: 大人您或许忘了，但我可是记得一清二楚！
t_en: Perhaps you may have forgotten, {M#sir}{F#ma'am}, but I remember like it was yesterday.

t_ch: 我做什么坏事被你记住了吗？
t_en: Was it something bad I did?

t_ch: 大人您玩笑了！您这种大侠怎么会做坏事呢？
t_en: You jest, esteemed friend! How can a noble hero like you do anything vile?

t_ch: 幽囚狱暴乱那日，狱中一片大乱，一批罪囚企图趁乱窜逃。
t_en: It was the day of the riot, the entire prison was in complete chaos, and an entire group of prisoners attempted to use the commotion as cover for their escape.

t_ch: 在这危急关头，我看到大人您挺身而出，先是左蹬腿踢飞了一个药王残党，然后从背后掏出一根硕大的球棒，对着右前方那名步离狼卒就是一记痛击！
t_en: Just at that crucial moment, you bravely put your life on the line and sent a Sanctus Medicus disciple to your left flying with a mighty kick. Not a second later, you pulled a great baseball bat from your back and unleashed a mighty swing at the Borisin Wolftrooper in front of you!

t_ch: 永巷滔滔不绝地描述着你在幽囚狱的英勇战斗。其中的很多细节你自己都不记得，搞不好是他乱编的。
t_en: Yongxiang continues to describe your valiant battle in The Shackling Prison. Included are a great many details that even you don't remember. You suspect he might be making some of this up.

t_ch: 一个时辰后
t_en: One dual-hour later.

t_ch: 没曾想正当此时，竟又有更多的逃犯涌了过来！而您临危不乱，大吼一句「 规则，就是用来打破的！」挥舞着球棒，挡住了潮水般的暴徒……
t_en: ...but unexpectedly, even more escapees rushed over at that moment! Yet, you stood resolute amidst the chaos, loudly proclaiming "Rules, are made to be broken!" Then, you brought your bat to bear, and halted the rushing mob like a dam bearing the weight of a wave...

t_ch: 哎呀，糟糕…一不小心就讲上头了…抱歉，这些事您肯定比我更清楚，完全没必要浪费时间听我在这里喋喋不休……
t_en: Ah! Whoops! Got carried away. Sorry. You must know these details better than I do. There was no need to waste your time with all my incessant chattering...

t_ch: 我只是想亲口告诉您…您战斗的身姿非常帅气，我这辈子都不会忘记。
t_en: I just wanted to tell you that... Your battle posture was incredible. Awesome. I will never forget how you looked for as long as I live.

t_ch: 我自己记得都没你清楚！
t_en: You remember what I did better than I do!

t_ch: 您过奖了。这只不过是我唯一的长处罢了。
t_en: You flatter me. This is but my only strength.

t_ch: 你真没有添油加醋？
t_en: Are you sure you haven't embellished anything?

t_ch: 当然没有了！我记事就像刀子刻在石头上一样，绝不会出错。
t_en: Of course not! My memory is like markings left by a blade upon stone. Not one detail is wrong.

t_ch: 你是什么人肉录像机？
t_en: Are you some kind of biological recorder?

t_ch: 哈哈～我小时候参加选秀节目时，节目组确实就用的是「人肉录像机」这个称号。
t_en: Haha! I participated in a talent show as a child. The crew of that show did give me the title of "biological recorder."

t_ch: 刚才我说，「这辈子都不会忘记」…那并非是对于您的奉承之语，而是关于我的客观事实。从出生到现在，我从未忘记过任何事。
t_en: When I said "I would never forget" as long as I live earlier... That wasn't meant as a compliment to you, but an objective statement about me. From the time I was born, I have never forgotten a single thing.

t_ch: 好啦！不谈我的事了。您来找我这幽囚狱主簿，一定是有什么事吧？若是有什么想问的事，请尽管开口。
t_en: Alright! Enough about me, personally: You're here to see the Shackling Prison's secretary, right? There must be something you need. If you have a question, feel free to ask.

t_ch: 有些事情想问你。
t_en: There's something I'd like to ask you.

t_ch: 您请问吧。
t_en: Ask away.

t_ch: 幽囚狱主簿都要做什么？
t_en: What does a secretary of the Shackling Prison do?

t_ch: 哈哈，「主簿」这职称是不是听起来很厉害？其实呢，我就是幽囚狱的「笔杆子」。
t_en: Haha. Doesn't the title of "secretary" sound impressive? In actuality, I am the Shackling Prison's "penholder."

t_ch: 别看判官们逮捕凶犯时雷厉风行，其实背后都要走一套严谨的行政流程。就算是遇到紧急情况，现场做出了「处置」，事后也得补一个行政手续，我就是专门做这事的。
t_en: Though you've seen the majesty of judges arresting people left and right, hitting culprits with the full force of the law as they please. In actual fact, they have to follow a strict set of administrative procedures behind the scenes. Even if there is an emergency, and they have to deliver some on-site "judgment," there are still processes that have to be retroactively filed. This is what I specialize in.

t_ch: 另外还有些散碎的工作，比如判决文书、官方通告、内宣材料…反正在这幽囚狱里，只要是需要「写作」的地方，就是我的职责范围。
t_en: Additionally, there are miscellaneous tasks like issue verdicts, official announcements, internal marketing... So long as it has to do with "writing" in the Shackling Prison, it falls under my jurisdiction of work.

t_ch: 平时工作强度大吗？
t_en: Is work usually stressful?

t_ch: 还可以吧。主簿这个工作，其实说重要吧也没那么重要，但是必须有人做这件事。
t_en: It's acceptable. Perhaps the role of a secretary isn't that important in itself, but someone has to do this work.

t_ch: 毕竟公文系统是联盟法政体系的重要组成部分，格式和措辞都大有讲究，有时候一字之差就会酿成重大行政事故，没受过专业训练的人写不来。
t_en: Official documentation is an extremely important part of the alliance's legal system. The style and terminology are extremely particular, and sometimes just a small difference in wording can lead to a massive administrative accident. Anyone without formal training wouldn't be able to compose them.

t_ch: 不过幽囚狱的主簿也有特殊的地方——虽然工作强度不高，但给我们这些笔杆子的心理压力可不低。
t_en: Though, being a secretary in the Shackling Prison also has its unique aspects. The workload might not be that intense, but the pressure for us pen-holders is pretty substantial.

t_ch: 毕竟这里关押的都是些穷凶极恶的家伙，那些与他们相关的文书，往往都写满了常人难以想象的邪恶与残酷。
t_en: After all, these are black-hearted criminals we're holding here. The documents related to them are all filled with cruelty and atrocities beyond the fathoming of a normal person.

t_ch: 而偏偏我还是个过目不忘的人。那一桩桩一件件惨案，对我来说，时时刻刻都犹在眼前。
t_en: And I am someone who doesn't forget. Each and every tragic case is carved into my mind's eye, there every single moment.

t_ch: 你的记忆力为什么这么好？
t_en: Why is your memory so good?

t_ch: 天生的。当我父母发现我这项天赋之后，我就成了我们家的摇钱树。出演各种节目、代言各种广告、客串各种幻戏…我一度是「罗浮」最有名的小孩。
t_en: I was born with it. After my parents discovered my talent, I became my family's breadwinner. I joined talent shows, participated in advertisements, made guest appearances on all kinds of immersias... I was once a pretty famous kid on the Luofu.

t_ch: 但三百多年过去，我也长大了，观众也看腻了，我也就做不成摇钱树了，唉…后来我就和家里闹掰了。没办法，对他们而言，我已经没有价值了。
t_en: But three hundred years went by, and I grew up. The audience got bored, and I wasn't that valuable anymore... *sigh* Then, I got estranged from my family. There's nothing for it. To them, I'm worthless.

t_ch: 三百多岁的人，没有「记忆力好」以外的一技之长，没有值得依靠的社会关系，甚至没有一个…能回去的家……
t_en: For a three hundred-year-old person with no skills beyond having a "good memory," no social connections on which they could rely, not even a home to return to...

t_ch: 一转眼，能容下我的地方，也就只剩下十王司了——他们就喜欢和社会脱钩的人…不好意思，我好像聊跑题了，哈哈。
t_en: In a blink, the only place that would take me was the Ten-Lords Commission. They love people disconnected from society... Sorry, I seem to have gone off-topic, haha.

t_ch: 不过您看，十王司根本没有看上去那么可怕。恐怕整个银河，也只有十王司愿意接纳像我这种无法融入社会的人了。
t_en: But as you see, the Ten-Lords Commission isn't that scary. I'm afraid in all the cosmos, only they will accept a social outcast like me.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的，大人若还有问题随时找我。
t_en: Very well. If you have any more questions, please come and find me.

t_ch: 暂时没什么事。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 好的，大人若还有问题随时找我。
t_en: Very well. If you have any more questions, please come and find me.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大人，有什么需要吗？在这幽囚狱里，绝对没人比我记忆力好。若是有用得到的地方，在下义不容辞。
t_en: Do you still need anything else, {NICKNAME}? You will find no one in The Shackling Prison with a better memory than me. If I can be of any use to you, please do not hesitate to ask.

t_ch: 哎，这么点钱怎么够花啊，再不开工的话，家用怎么办……
t_en: Oh, how is this enough money for the whole family? We'll go broke if I don't get work soon...

t_ch: 诶，这位老板！老板好，看您面生，是头一回来「罗浮」吧？我瞧您人生地不熟的，现在到处都人心惶惶的，取货的话恐怕……
t_en: Hey, look at this bigshot boss over here! Hello, boss, is this your first time aboard the Luofu? You look like a stranger in a strange land, and there's panic in the streets. If you're here to pick up some stock...

t_ch: 不过，您放心，我在这流云渡上工作了几十年，再复杂的手续也难不倒我，尤其是货物报关清关和装卸的事项。
t_en: But don't you worry! I've worked in Cloudford for decades. Doesn't matter how complicated the bureaucracy is, nothing is an issue for me, especially things like customs clearance and unloading goods.

t_ch: 您看，你需不需要——
t_en: Look, do you need any—

t_ch: 不需要，我不是生意人。
t_en: No thanks. I'm no merchant.

t_ch: 抱歉，我只是路过的。
t_en: Sorry. I'm just passing through.

t_ch: 没关系，反正今天我还没开单，现在闲着也是闲着。您要是感兴趣，我可以跟您介绍一下我的业务——我敢说罗浮上，没人比我更精通货物装卸的事项了。
t_en: No worries. Anyway, I haven't even made a sale yet today and I'm just chilling. If you like, I can tell you a little bit about my work. And I'll bet there's nobody else aboard the Luofu who's better at handling freight than me.

t_ch: 虽说现在您用不着，但以后万一有需要了，指不定就有用到我的地方了，嘿嘿。
t_en: You might not need it now, but just in case there's some time in the future when I could help you out... haha!

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 您尽管问吧，这流云渡上下的手续我可熟啦！
t_en: Feel free to ask anything. I know my way around all the freight handling procedures in Cloudford!

t_ch: 你这业务范围挺广啊？
t_en: Your scope of operations sounds pretty broad, huh?

t_ch: 那当然，平时流云渡的货运量大，竞争也大，要讨口饭吃可没这么容易。
t_en: Sure is. Cloudford is a high traffic port but it's very competitive, so it's tough to make a living here.

t_ch: 我的业务自然不止装卸集装箱这么简单，什么货物入港、清关报关、货物检查的，这些该走的手续我都可以代办。
t_en: Naturally, I don't just handle packing and unloading — that's far too simple. I also handle all kinds of procedures, like cargo entry, customs clearance, and quarantine inspections.

t_ch: 当然，每个老板的实际情况都不一样，具体要做些什么，这就得视乎老板们的需求了。
t_en: But of course, every customer has their own custom order. The specifics of what I actually do are governed by the needs of the customer.

t_ch: 能介绍下你的工作吗？
t_en: Can you tell me a little bit about your work?

t_ch: 货物装卸这事吧，说起来简单，无非是用机巧臂操纵下集装箱什么的。但实践起来，里面的学问可不少。我也是几十年下来天天干这事儿，这才练出了名头。
t_en: Handling freight — packing and unloading — sounds simple enough, right? It's just containers being moved around by a big mechanical crane. But in practice, it takes a lot of learning to do. I've been doing this day-in, day-out for decades. That's just how long it takes to earn a reputation for it.

t_ch: 至于我，业内都知道我这人做事是最心细的。但凡开工，我都会遵循着严格的安全标准来，这样对人对己都好。
t_en: As for me, everyone in the business knows I'm the most careful person out there. Every time I start a project, I'll strictly follow safety guidelines and procedures. That typically works out best for both me and the customer.

t_ch: 不信的话，您可以去码头上打听打听，我的货物损毁率绝对是业内最低的！
t_en: Why, if you don't believe me, then just ask around over at the jetty. My damaged goods rate is easily the lowest in all of Cloudford!

t_ch: 这种时候你还出来接单？
t_en: What are you doing out taking orders at this time?

t_ch: 最近确实挺危险的，但没办法呀，养家糊口的事怎么能耽搁。我家里那么多张嘴要养呢，我要是不工作，那就真是手停口停了。
t_en: It's been pretty rough out there recently, but there's nothing I can do. I've got a family to support who can't just wait it out! With so many mouths to feed back home, I can't just stop working. No work means no income.

t_ch: 都是成年人啦，甭管您是化外民，还是我们仙舟本地人，都得为生计而努力嘛！何况这种事…咱们也没得选不是吗？
t_en: We're both adults. You understand, right? Whether you're an Outworlder or a Xianzhou native, we all need to earn a living! ...It's not like we have a choice in the matter, right?

t_ch: 为什么不让自动机巧来干？
t_en: Why don't you use an ingenium to automatically handle freight for you?

t_ch: 有台机器能把我干的活全都包圆了，还干的又快又好，以仙舟的技术早就能办到了。那为什么你还能在这儿见到我呢？好好想想，孩子。
t_en: Xianzhou mastered the technology a long time ago to kit out any ingenium to do everything as quickly and as well as I do it. So then, what am I still around for, huh? Think about it, kid.

t_ch: 让机器把活全干了，我又该干什么呢，胡思乱想？那样一来我不就变成仙舟的不安定因素了？明白了吧？
t_en: If a machine does this job for me, what am I going to do? Just sit there idling the day away? Why, I'd slowly become a destabilizing factor for the Xianzhou, wouldn't I? Are you starting to catch my drift?

t_ch: 再说了，自动机巧它也不会给地衡司纳税啊。
t_en: Or to put it another way, how much tax would an automatic ingenium pay to the Realm-Keeping Commission? Nothing.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的！
t_en: Okay!

t_ch: 我没什么要问的了。
t_en: I've got no questions to ask.

t_ch: 老板，如果您需要一个可靠的、经验丰富的货运工人，请务必考虑我，我会竭尽全力干活的！
t_en: Hey boss, if you need a reliable and experienced freight handler, then please keep me in mind. I'll always give it my best!

t_ch: 总之，您有需求的话请随时来找我，我会给您打折的！
t_en: So if you have any orders, come find me any time. I'll even cut you a deal!

t_ch: 老板好！老板您需要帮忙吗？支持各类货运业务代办，现在下单还有折扣哦！
t_en: Hey boss! Need any help there? We support all kinds of freight needs. We're also having special discounts on orders now!

t_ch: 啊…是您啊，打扰啦，我今天还没开单呢，反正闲着也是闲着…您要是感兴趣，我可以跟您介绍一下我的业务——我敢说罗浮上，没人比我更精通货物装卸的事项了。
t_en: Ah, It's you. I haven't even made a sale yet today and I'm just chilling. If you like, I can tell you a little bit about my work. And I'll bet there's nobody else aboard the Luofu who's better at handling freight than me.

t_ch: 虽说现在您用不着，但以后万一有需要了，指不定就有用到我的地方了，嘿嘿。
t_en: You might not need it now, but just in case there's some time in the future when I could help you out... haha!

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 您尽管问吧，这流云渡上下的手续我可熟啦！
t_en: Feel free to ask anything. I know my way around all the freight handling procedures in Cloudford!

t_ch: 那当然，平时流云渡的货运量大，竞争也大，要讨口饭吃可没这么容易。
t_en: Sure is. Cloudford is a high traffic port but it's very competitive, so it's tough to make a living here.

I've been doing this day-in, day-out for decades. That's just how long it takes to earn a reputation for it.

t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的！
t_en: Okay!

t_ch: 我没什么要问的了。
t_en: I've got no questions to ask.

t_ch: 老板，如果您需要一个可靠的、经验丰富的集装箱装卸工，请务必考虑我，我会竭尽全力干活的！
t_en: Hey boss, if you need a reliable and experienced professional to load and unload containers, please keep me in mind. I'll always give it my best!

t_ch: 总之，您有需求的话请随时来找我，我会给您打折的！
t_en: So if you have any orders, come find me any time. I'll even cut you a deal!

t_ch: 有些事情想问你。
t_en: There's something I'd like to ask you.

t_ch: 您请问吧。
t_en: Ask away.

t_ch: 幽囚狱主簿都要做什么？
t_en: What does a secretary of the Shackling Prison do?

t_ch: 哈哈，「主簿」这职称是不是听起来很厉害？其实呢，我就是幽囚狱的「笔杆子」。
t_en: Haha. Doesn't the title of "secretary" sound impressive? In actuality, I am the Shackling Prison's "penholder."

t_ch: 别看判官们逮捕凶犯时雷厉风行，其实背后都要走一套严谨的行政流程。就算是遇到紧急情况，现场做出了「处置」，事后也得补一个行政手续，我就是专门做这事的。
t_en: Though you've seen the majesty of judges arresting people left and right, hitting culprits with the full force of the law as they please. In actual fact, they have to follow a strict set of administrative procedures behind the scenes. Even if there is an emergency, and they have to deliver some on-site "judgment," there are still processes that have to be retroactively filed. This is what I specialize in.

t_ch: 另外还有些散碎的工作，比如判决文书、官方通告、内宣材料…反正在这幽囚狱里，只要是需要「写作」的地方，就是我的职责范围。
t_en: Additionally, there are miscellaneous tasks like issue verdicts, official announcements, internal marketing... So long as it has to do with "writing" in the Shackling Prison, it falls under my jurisdiction of work.

t_ch: 平时工作强度大吗？
t_en: Is work usually stressful?

t_ch: 还可以吧。主簿这个工作，其实说重要吧也没那么重要，但是必须有人做这件事。
t_en: It's acceptable. Perhaps the role of a secretary isn't that important in itself, but someone has to do this work.

t_ch: 毕竟公文系统是联盟法政体系的重要组成部分，格式和措辞都大有讲究，有时候一字之差就会酿成重大行政事故，没受过专业训练的人写不来。
t_en: Official documentation is an extremely important part of the alliance's legal system. The style and terminology are extremely particular, and sometimes just a small difference in wording can lead to a massive administrative accident. Anyone without formal training wouldn't be able to compose them.

t_ch: 不过幽囚狱的主簿也有特殊的地方——虽然工作强度不高，但给我们这些笔杆子的心理压力可不低。
t_en: Though, being a secretary in the Shackling Prison also has its unique aspects. The workload might not be that intense, but the pressure for us pen-holders is pretty substantial.

t_ch: 毕竟这里关押的都是些穷凶极恶的家伙，那些与他们相关的文书，往往都写满了常人难以想象的邪恶与残酷。
t_en: After all, these are black-hearted criminals we're holding here. The documents related to them are all filled with cruelty and atrocities beyond the fathoming of a normal person.

t_ch: 而偏偏我还是个过目不忘的人。那一桩桩一件件惨案，对我来说，时时刻刻都犹在眼前。
t_en: And I am someone who doesn't forget. Each and every tragic case is carved into my mind's eye, there every single moment.

t_ch: 你的记忆力为什么这么好？
t_en: Why is your memory so good?

t_ch: 天生的。当我父母发现我这项天赋之后，我就成了我们家的摇钱树。出演各种节目、代言各种广告、客串各种幻戏…我一度是「罗浮」最有名的小孩。
t_en: I was born with it. After my parents discovered my talent, I became my family's breadwinner. I joined talent shows, participated in advertisements, made guest appearances on all kinds of immersias... I was once a pretty famous kid on the Luofu.

t_ch: 但三百多年过去，我也长大了，观众也看腻了，我也就做不成摇钱树了，唉…后来我就和家里闹掰了。没办法，对他们而言，我已经没有价值了。
t_en: But three hundred years went by, and I grew up. The audience got bored, and I wasn't that valuable anymore... *sigh* Then, I got estranged from my family. There's nothing for it. To them, I'm worthless.

t_ch: 三百多岁的人，没有「记忆力好」以外的一技之长，没有值得依靠的社会关系，甚至没有一个…能回去的家……
t_en: For a three hundred-year-old person with no skills beyond having a "good memory," no social connections on which they could rely, not even a home to return to...

t_ch: 一转眼，能容下我的地方，也就只剩下十王司了——他们就喜欢和社会脱钩的人…不好意思，我好像聊跑题了，哈哈。
t_en: In a blink, the only place that would take me was the Ten-Lords Commission. They love people disconnected from society... Sorry, I seem to have gone off-topic, haha.

t_ch: 不过您看，十王司根本没有看上去那么可怕。恐怕整个银河，也只有十王司愿意接纳像我这种无法融入社会的人了。
t_en: But as you see, the Ten-Lords Commission isn't that scary. I'm afraid in all the cosmos, only they will accept a social outcast like me.

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 好的，大人若还有问题随时找我。
t_en: Very well. If you have any more questions, please come and find me.

t_ch: 暂时没什么事。
t_en: That's all for now.

t_ch: 好的，大人若还有问题随时找我。
t_en: Very well. If you have any more questions, please come and find me.

t_ch: 唷，这不是仙舟稀客嘛，怎么有时间有闲专程来太卜司视察工作呀？
t_en: Hey, if it isn't a sight on the Xianzhou. How have you found time to make a dedicated trip to check out the work of the Divination Commission?

t_ch: 要我说这里游手好闲的人还挺多，您来找我，最好是真的有什么要紧事，我忙得很。
t_en: If you ask me, there're too many lazybones hanging out here. You'd better have something urgent if you're looking for me, because I'm buried in work.

t_ch: 我看你也不是很忙的样子。
t_en: You don't look very busy.

t_ch: 我看您也不是卜者，就不必外行指导内行了。
t_en: I don't think you're a diviner, buddy. So stay in your lane, alright?

t_ch: 我并不是对您不满，但是您显然缺乏对占卜演算的了解…建议您不要把青雀当作太卜司的职场参考，那是特例。
t_en: I'm not upset with you. It's just that you obviously have no comprehension of how divination works... I don't suggest holding up Qingque as a model worker at the Divination Commission, because she is an absolute outlier.

t_ch: 我跟您说这干嘛，那只小麻雀最近总是被太卜呼来喝去的，我已经不打算埋怨她了。
t_en: Why am I telling you all this? Because that little sparrow has been getting chewed out recently by the master diviner, so I don't intend on blaming her any further.

t_ch: 谁是闲人？我可得批评一番。
t_en: Who do you mean by "lazybones"? I'll have to give them a good talking-to.

t_ch: 您的脑海里都没有浮现起一张面孔吗？个子小小的，没事儿总在玩牌的那个……
t_en: Isn't there someone who pops into your mind straight away? Short, always playing tiles...

t_ch: 不过，我看那只小麻雀最近总是被太卜呼来喝去的，我已经不打算埋怨她了。
t_en: Anyway, it looks like that little sparrow has been getting chewed out recently by the master diviner, so I don't intend on blaming her any further.

t_ch: 我要说的，是那些身居要职，却在尸位素餐的人。呵呵，当然我不能说是谁。
t_en: What I'm talking about are higher-ups wasting space and money. Haha, of course I can't name names.

t_ch: 你说话可太冲了，我没惹你吧。
t_en: That's a little too harsh. Did I say something wrong?

t_ch: 这我得向您道歉，是我不对。您来的不时候，我才批评完司内职场风气，气还没消退…是有些口无遮拦了。
t_en: Sorry, it's my bad. When you came, I was just beating up on our workplace culture, and I still hadn't cooled off... Sorry for running my mouth off.

t_ch: 哎，要是太卜司的人都能像您这样，我也不会生这么大气了。
t_en: Ah, if everyone at the Divination Commission were like you, I wouldn't have gotten so worked up about things.

t_ch: 好了好了，我就这么一说，您就这么一听，我说话也没过脑子，请您也别往心里去。我这人气性就这样，送您一点儿小礼物，我会改正态度的。
t_en: Alright alright, I'm just letting off some steam and rambling here. Don't take me too seriously. It's just how I am! So why don't you hold onto this? It's a little gift, and it'll get me feeling better.

t_ch: 说吧，你肯定是有什么事情来找我的吧？
t_en: Go on, you must have come looking for me for a reason, right?

t_ch: 我其实没什么事，只是想聊聊…
t_en: Actually, it's nothing. Really, I just come to chat...

t_ch: 您都这样说了，我也不好驳您的面子，聊吧。
t_en: If that's the case, how can I refuse? Chat away.

t_ch: 你好像对职场氛围颇有微词。
t_en: You seem to be pretty critical about the workplace culture here.

t_ch: 您说的没错。这建木生发之后，太卜司内全力运作，可以说是忙得手忙脚乱。
t_en: That's right. Since the Ambrosial Arbor's new growth, we've been firing on all cylinders here at the Divination Commission. Just flat out busy.

t_ch: 太卜大人临危受命，有要务在身，这司内上下事宜，卜官们自当殷勤打理。每个人都应该对自己应该完成的工作心中有数，而不是等待着别人催促。
t_en: The master diviner is trying to manage the situation as best as she can with so many important tasks at hand. The managing diviners should be working hard to take care of their own departments. Everyone should know what they need for their roles and shouldn't need other people to keep hounding them.

t_ch: 有的卜官没有那个能力，非得要做做样子。做事缺乏前瞻后顾，张口便说「可以做、可以做」。等到该验收工作的时候，又会说「我觉得这个没有必要」……
t_en: Some managing diviners lack this capacity though, and so they just go through the motions. Completely lacking in foresight or retrospection, just blindly saying "Yes, okay" to everything. But when it comes time to turn the work in, they'll say, "I don't think that's necessary"...

t_ch: 口说无凭，得让那人立下字据。
t_en: Verbal confirmations mean nothing. They should be made to put their promises in writing.

t_ch: 你说的没错，我现在已经能熟练掌握这项技能了。
t_en: You're not wrong. I've come to master this trick.

t_ch: 每次对接工作，我都会要求保留交流记录…事后证明，这样做切实地提高了沟通效率，我正建议整个司部里推广呢。
t_en: Every time I hand over any work, I'll keep a record of all communication... It's been proven to improve communication efficiency, and I'm in the process of recommending the entire commission to adopt this practice.

t_ch: 多说无益，得让那人当众道歉。
t_en: There's no use in just talking about it. Make them issue a public apology.

t_ch: 你是没见过人脸皮厚的时候。
t_en: You have no idea how shameless some people can get.

t_ch: 这算是给你一个职场小建议吧，主动保留你们一切的交流记录，这会省去一切「记不清了」、「我没说过」之类的麻烦。
t_en: This is just a small workplace tip for you: Be proactive about recording all your communications. This will cut straight through anyone trying to say "I don't remember" or "I didn't say that."

t_ch: 互相包容，你们还得一起共事。
t_en: Show tolerance to each other. After all, you still have to work together.

t_ch: 很难想象你经历过怎样的职场，要么你很幸运，同事间相处愉快；要么你已经是身经百战的老油条，职场上发生的一切你都能接受。
t_en: It's hard to imagine what your workplace experience has been like. Either you're lucky enough to get on with your colleagues, or you're a seasoned veteran who's capable of accepting anything thrown at them at the workplace.

t_ch: 你要真是这样，我很羡慕你。
t_en: If you really are that kind of person, then I have to say I envy you very much.

t_ch: 虽然太卜大人虽然嘴上没说，但她老人家早就有打算治治这里的歪风邪气了。我也不瞎操心了，咱们就等着瞧吧。
t_en: The master diviner may not have said anything, but she has long held plans for cleaning up negative work culture here. So I don't bother worrying about it anymore. Let's just wait and see what happens.

t_ch: 你好像和青雀很熟的样子？
t_en: You seem pretty close to Qingque.

t_ch: 呵呵，我俩很熟？您真是说笑了，我上班的时候基本见不着她人，我可没有自信说「我和青雀很熟」。
t_en: Hah, us? Close? You must be a comedian or something. I hardly ever see her at work, so I can't possibly say that we're "close."

t_ch: 我记得你和青雀打过几次交道。你又是怎么看她的呢？
t_en: I remember you dealing with Qingque a couple of times before. What do you think of her?

t_ch: 可可爱爱，人畜无害。
t_en: She's cute and wouldn't hurt a fly.

t_ch: 确实挺可爱的。
t_en: She is actually pretty cute.

t_ch: 随处可见，平平无奇。
t_en: Everything about her is utterly ordinary.

t_ch: 确实有一种不引人注目的感觉。
t_en: She does feel rather inconspicuous, doesn't she?

t_ch: 阴险狡诈，难以靠近。
t_en: She's sneaky, cunning, and unreliable.

t_ch: …你说的真是青雀吗？
t_en: ...Are we still talking about Qingque here?

t_ch: 那只小麻雀想尽办法将自己不惹人瞩目，但在我看来，这是弄巧成拙——有谁能永远只做到及格分呢？这不是有意为之，又是什么呢？
t_en: That little sparrow has tried her best to stay out of the spotlight, but in my estimation this has only backfired on her. Who out there is capable of only doing just enough every time? If it's not intentional, how else can we explain it?

t_ch: 再说了，我都能察觉，太卜能不察觉吗？
t_en: Besides, if someone like me can already see through her, could it possibly have escaped the master diviner's eyes?

t_ch: 现在主要在忙些什么呢？
t_en: What are you keeping yourself busy with these days?

t_ch: 卜算，依旧是卜算。具体内容，请恕我不能透露。
t_en: Divinations. Still more divinations. Please forgive me for not divulging any further details.

t_ch: 说真的，我刚才差点就没过脑子就直接讲给你听了。事关太卜司的高压线，我应该更加谨慎一些的…现在想起来，还有点后怕。
t_en: To be honest, I almost just rattled all that off to you without even thinking things through. I should be more careful when it comes to top secret info about the Divination Commission... Thinking about it now, I'm starting to get a little uneasy.

t_ch: 你就别打听了，我是卜官，保密是我的职业道德。
t_en: Okay, enough questions. I'm a managing diviner and keeping trade secrets is a part of my professional ethics.

t_ch: 我就不打扰了，您接着忙。
t_en: I won't bother you any further. Please carry on with your work.

t_ch: 太卜大人好清静，您也别在这里到处打听了。再见，不送。
t_en: The master diviner likes her peace and quiet, and you should stop asking so many questions around here. So long for now. Please let yourself out.

t_ch: 我不打扰了，请继续演奏。
t_en: I won't bother you any further. Keep on playing.

t_ch: 太卜大人好清静，您也别在这里到处打听了。再见，不送。
t_en: The master diviner likes her peace and quiet, and you should stop asking so many questions around here. So long for now. Please let yourself out.

t_ch: 唷，明明这里有这么多闲人，偏偏来找我……
t_en: Hmph, why'd you have to pick me out of all these lazybones here...

t_ch: 说吧，又有什么要紧事了？
t_en: Go on then, what is it this time?

t_ch: 我其实没什么事，只是想聊聊…
t_en: Actually, it's nothing. Really, I just come to chat...

t_ch: 您都这样说了，我也不好驳您的面子，聊吧。
t_en: If that's the case, how can I refuse? Chat away.

t_ch: 你好像对职场氛围颇有微词。
t_en: You seem to be pretty critical about the workplace culture here.

t_ch: 您说的没错。这建木生发之后，太卜司内全力运作，可以说是忙得手忙脚乱。
t_en: That's right. Since the Ambrosial Arbor's new growth, we've been firing on all cylinders here at the Divination Commission. Just flat out busy.

t_ch: 我不打扰了，请继续演奏。
t_en: I won't bother you any further. Keep on playing.

t_ch: 太卜大人好清静，您也别在这里到处打听了。再见，不送。
t_en: The master diviner likes her peace and quiet, and you should stop asking so many questions around here. So long for now. Please let yourself out.

t_ch: 想和你打听一下静斋的事。
t_en: I'd like to ask you about Jingzhai.

t_ch: 我虽然是个卜者，但我不喜欢八卦别人的。
t_en: I'm a diviner, I shouldn't be gossiping.

t_ch: 女孩左右顾盼，见四下无人，把脸转向你，满眼兴奋。
t_en: The girl looked around. After making sure the coast was clear, she turned to you, her eyes filled with excitement.

t_ch: 静斋看着跟没事人似的，我愣是没看出有什么问题来，你们合起来瞒着外人呢？
t_en: Jingzhai looks fine to me, and I can't see any problems. Are you two keeping a secret?

t_ch: 前些日子你来找我打听过一回，今日又来…前不久还有个蛮帅气的男孩子过来找她…莫不是……
t_en: You came to ask about Jingzhai a few days back, and now you're here again... There was a good-looking guy who came for her too not long ago. Could it be...

t_ch: 我不好说…
t_en: I can't say for sure...

t_ch: 她的私事，我也不好瞎说。
t_en: I shouldn't gossip about her private matters.

t_ch: 嘁，你不肯说，那我也不知道。
t_en: Tsk, if you're not talking, I'm not telling you anything.

t_ch: 无非就是那些家长里短啊爱恨情仇的，无聊，别再来问了，我什么也不知道！
t_en: I bet it's just those boring familial news and romance. Boring! Stop asking me. I know nothing!

t_ch: 真搞不懂，这太卜司上下怎么就没个说人话的，就想着从我这儿套话，哎……
t_en: I just don't get it. Why does nobody at the Divination Commission speak normally? Everyone's always trying to trick me into saying something, sheesh...

t_ch: 太卜司的人情世故真是复杂，这检修工作到底什么时候才能做完呐？我好想回工造司去……
t_en: The Divination Commission has its own social norms with its own complex rules. When on earth are all the maintenance going to be finished? I can't wait to get back to the Artisanship Commission...

t_ch: 太卜司怎么你了？
t_en: What did the Divination Commission ever do to you?

t_ch: 这太卜司可真不是正常人待的地方，嚯，害我天天都头疼……
t_en: This is no place for normal people. Ahhh, it gives me a headache every single day...

t_ch: …呃，你别瞎想，我只是觉得他们说话太弯弯绕绕了，我大老粗一个，和他们实在是聊不来。
t_en: ...Uh, don't take what I said too literally. I just think they talk round and round in circles. I'm a hands-on tradesman and I just can't seem to level with them.

t_ch: 能有多复杂？
t_en: How complicated can it be?

t_ch: 普通玉兆里篆刻的是兆亿符箓，而这穷观阵就不止…噢你说的是「人情世故」啊。
t_en: A normal jade abacus contains a hundred million talismans, but the Matrix of Prescience has far more than that... Oh, you're talking about the "social norm" there, aren't you?

t_ch: 我哪儿知道啊，我要懂这个，我何至于夜夜都睡不好觉……
t_en: How would I know? I'd like to know, too. I haven't had a good night's sleep in forever...

t_ch: …好好的检修工作愣是扫兴至极，算了，不提这个了，这种人心莫测的事听多了胃疼。
t_en: ...All this good maintenance work has somehow become utterly demoralizing. Ah, forget it. I don't wanna talk about this anymore. These mysteries of the human cunning just give me an even bigger headache.

t_ch: 对了，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}，看你有些面生，也不像是咱们仙舟人，你是来参观的还是来找人的？
t_en: Oh yes, {F#young lady}{M#young man}, you don't look like you're from Xianzhou. Have you come to visit, or to see someone?

t_ch: 我正好路过，想跟你聊聊…
t_en: I was just passing by and wanted to stop in for a chat with you...

t_ch: 跟我聊聊？行啊，你有什么想知道的尽管问便是了。但我丑话说在前头，太卜司的事我了解的不多，你若是想打探什么消息，那还是请回吧。
t_en: A chat? With me? Alright, let me know if there's anything in particular you'd like to ask. But let me state up front. I don't know much about the Divination Commission, so if you're after intel, I'll stop you right there.

t_ch: 你好像很讨厌太卜司的人？
t_en: You seem to really hate these people at the Divination Commission, huh?

t_ch: 「讨厌」？真不至于，我只是不习惯这里的工作氛围而已。就好比你们化外民来到仙舟，总有人说自己「水土不服」，换个环境多少有点不适应。
t_en: "Hate"? That's a strong word. I'm just unaccustomed to the work culture here. Just like when you Outworlders come aboard the Xianzhou and people say you "don't get the culture." If you take anyone out of their natural environment, they'd be uncomfortable to a certain degree.

t_ch: 举个例子，我平时负些检修工作，可和我对接的几位卜官各个都有颗玲珑心，说话恨不得绕几百个圈子。
t_en: For example, I normally do maintenance work here, but the managing diviners I interact with are all so crafty, that they just beat around the bush all day without making anything clear.

t_ch: 玉兆算端有时会技术性故障，运气不好的难免会中招。某些卜官就换着法儿来问我，什么「不见某某卜官上报故障，莫不是压根没启动卜算呀」之类的，我哪儿知道啊。
t_en: A jade abacus calculation terminal can sometimes have technical faults, and if you're unlucky, your terminal of the day would be the one to malfunction. So some managing diviners try to ask me leading questions like "diviner So-and-so hasn't been reporting any faults. Does that mean they never performed their divination at all?" Heck if I know.

t_ch: 坏了就坏了，没坏就没坏，在我们工造司都是一句话的事，怎么还能扯出这么多有的没的啊？哎，我这嘴又笨，只得愣在那儿张着嘴「阿吧阿吧」半天讲不出话，丢死人了。
t_en: If it's broken, it's broken. Otherwise, it's fine. That's all we need to say at the Artisanship Commission. So why do they keep blathering on about all this other unrelated stuff? And I'm not good with words either, so I just stand there stuttering and can't get my point across. It's just so embarrassing.

t_ch: 工造司怎么会派你来这儿？
t_en: Why did the Artisanship Commission send you over here?

t_ch: 我也不知道啊，上头说要我来那我就来了呗。哎，说到这个就来气。
t_en: Your guess is as good as mine. If the higher-ups say they want me here, then I'm here. *sigh* Talking about this is starting to make me angry again.

t_ch: 我刚来这边例行检修穷观阵的时候，某位卜官一会儿问我「认不认识明阅」，一会儿又问我「你和绘星大人是什么关系」。
t_en: When I first came here for a routine inspection on the Matrix of Prescience, a managing diviner here asked me, "Do you know Mingyue?" Then asked me again, "What is your relationship with Huixing?"!

t_ch: 我就不明白了，咱们工造司做事都照足流程来，该例行询问记录的我总不能漏吧？跟负责这些事儿的卜官聊几句不是很正常吗？
t_en: I don't get it. We all do stuff by the book over at the Artisanship Commission, and I can't skip out on routine inspection and records, right? Isn't it normal enough to have a few words with the managing diviner about these documents?

t_ch: 我在太卜司压根就没有认识的人，真搞不懂他们怎么会这么问，简直像十王司的问询一样，恨不得连小时候认识谁都要从你嘴里挖出来……
t_en: I don't know anybody at the Divination Commission at all. I have no idea why they'd ask me such a question. It's just like an interrogation by the Ten-Lords Commission. They'll even grill you about who you were hanging out with in childhood...

t_ch: 有什么内幕消息吗？
t_en: Do you have the lowdown on the situation?

t_ch: 没！有！再说一遍，没有内幕消息。
t_en: Nope! To re-iterate, I don't have any inside intel.

t_ch: …太卜司这地儿莫不是有点问题，天天都有人问我有什么「最新消息」「内幕消息」的。这究竟是有什么风声了？你们倒是仔细说说，别光问我啊！
t_en: ...Could it be that there's something wrong with the Divination Commission? People keep asking me day in day out whether I have the "latest news" or "inside scoop." What kind of talk is this? Just be straight up with me, and stop asking all these questions!

t_ch: 算了，我没什么想问的了。
t_en: Forget it. I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 嗯好…哦对了，差点忘了，你要是来参观穷观阵的，你远远地看一眼就好，未经太卜大人许可，外人可是不得靠近的。
t_en: Fine... Oh yes, I almost forget. If you come and visit the Matrix of Prescience, please view it from a distance. Outsiders are forbidden from approaching it without permission from the master diviner.

t_ch: 好了，没什么事的话以后再聊吧，我手里还有一大堆活儿没做完呢。
t_en: Okay, if there's nothing else, then let's talk again later. I've still got a mountain of work to get through.

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'd better leave you to it.

t_ch: 嗯好…哦对了，差点忘了，你要是来参观穷观阵的，你远远地看一眼就好，未经太卜大人许可，外人可是不准靠近的。

t_ch: 好了，没什么事的话以后再聊吧，我手里还有一大堆活儿没做完呢。
t_en: Okay, if there's nothing else, then let's talk again later. I've still got a mountain of work to get through.

t_ch: 咦，{F#小姑娘}{M#小兄弟}，又是你啊？你来这儿有什么事儿吗，是来参观的还是来找人的？
t_en: Hey, {F#young lady}{M#young man}, what are you doing back here? Have you come on business? Or to visit or see someone?

t_ch: 我正好路过，想跟你聊聊…
t_en: I was just passing by and wanted to stop in for a chat with you...

t_ch: 跟我聊聊？行啊，你有什么想知道的尽管问便是了。但我丑话说在前头，太卜司的事我了解的不多，你若是想打探什么消息，那还是请回吧。
t_en: A chat? With me? Alright, let me know if there's anything in particular you'd like to ask. But let me state up front. I don't know much about the Divination Commission, so if you're after intel, I'll stop you right there.

t_ch: 你好像很讨厌太卜司的人？
t_en: You seem to really hate these people at the Divination Commission, huh?

t_ch: 「讨厌」？真不至于，我只是不习惯这里的工作氛围而已。就好比你们化外民来到仙舟，总有人说自己「水土不服」，换个环境多少有点不适应。
t_en: "Hate"? That's a strong word. I'm just unaccustomed to the work culture here. Just like when you Outworlders come aboard the Xianzhou and people say you "don't get the culture." If you take anyone out of their natural environment, they'd be uncomfortable to a certain degree.

t_ch: 举个例子，我平时负些检修工作，可和我对接的几位卜官各个都有颗玲珑心，说话恨不得绕几百个圈子。
t_en: For example, I normally do maintenance work here, but the managing diviners I interact with are all so crafty, that they just beat around the bush all day without making anything clear.

t_ch: 玉兆算端有时会技术性故障，运气不好的难免会中招。某些卜官就换着法儿来问我，什么「不见某某卜官上报故障，莫不是压根没启动卜算呀」之类的，我哪儿知道啊。
t_en: A jade abacus calculation terminal can sometimes have technical faults, and if you're unlucky, your terminal of the day would be the one to malfunction. So some managing diviners try to ask me leading questions like "diviner So-and-so hasn't been reporting any faults. Does that mean they never performed their divination at all?" Heck if I know.

t_ch: 坏了就坏了，没坏就没坏，在我们工造司都是一句话的事，怎么还能扯出这么多有的没的啊？哎，我这嘴又笨，只得愣在那儿张着嘴「阿吧阿吧」半天讲不出话，丢死人了。
t_en: If it's broken, it's broken. Otherwise, it's fine. That's all we need to say at the Artisanship Commission. So why do they keep blathering on about all this other unrelated stuff? And I'm not good with words either, so I just stand there stuttering and can't get my point across. It's just so embarrassing.

t_ch: 工造司怎么会派你来这儿？
t_en: Why did the Artisanship Commission send you over here?

t_ch: 我也不知道啊，上头说要我来那我就来了呗。哎，说到这个就来气。
t_en: Your guess is as good as mine. If the higher-ups say they want me here, then I'm here. *sigh* Talking about this is starting to make me angry again.

t_ch: 我刚来这边例行检修穷观阵的时候，某位卜官一会儿问我「认不认识明阅」，一会儿又问我「你和绘星大人是什么关系」。
t_en: When I first came here for a routine inspection on the Matrix of Prescience, a managing diviner here asked me, "Do you know Mingyue?" Then asked me again, "What is your relationship with Huixing?"!

t_ch: 我就不明白了，咱们工造司做事都照足流程来，该例行询问记录的我总不能漏吧？跟负责这些事儿的卜官聊几句不是很正常吗？
t_en: I don't get it. We all do stuff by the book over at the Artisanship Commission, and I can't skip out on routine inspection and records, right? Isn't it normal enough to have a few words with the managing diviner about these documents?

t_ch: 我在太卜司压根就没有认识的人，真搞不懂他们怎么会这么问，简直像十王司的问询一样，恨不得连小时候认识谁都要从你嘴里挖出来……
t_en: I don't know anybody at the Divination Commission at all. I have no idea why they'd ask me such a question. It's just like an interrogation by the Ten-Lords Commission. They'll even grill you about who you were hanging out with in childhood...

t_ch: 有什么内幕消息吗？
t_en: Do you have the lowdown on the situation?

t_ch: 没！有！再说一遍，没有内幕消息。
t_en: Nope! To re-iterate, I don't have any inside intel.

t_ch: …太卜司这地儿莫不是有点问题，天天都有人问我有什么「最新消息」「内幕消息」的。这究竟是有什么风声了？你们倒是仔细说说，别光问我啊！
t_en: ...Could it be that there's something wrong with the Divination Commission? People keep asking me day in day out whether I have the "latest news" or "inside scoop." What kind of talk is this? Just be straight up with me, and stop asking all these questions!

t_ch: 算了，我没什么想问的了。
t_en: Forget it. I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 嗯好…哦对了，差点忘了，你要是来参观穷观阵的，你远远地看一眼就好，未经太卜大人许可，外人可是不得靠近的。
t_en: Fine... Oh yes, I almost forget. If you come and visit the Matrix of Prescience, please view it from a distance. Outsiders are forbidden from approaching it without permission from the master diviner.

t_ch: 好了，没什么事的话以后再聊吧，我手里还有一大堆活儿没做完呢。
t_en: Okay, if there's nothing else, then let's talk again later. I've still got a mountain of work to get through.

t_ch: 还是不打扰你了。
t_en: I'd better leave you to it.

t_ch: 嗯好…哦对了，差点忘了，你要是来参观穷观阵的，你远远地看一眼就好，未经太卜大人许可，外人可是不准靠近的。
t_en: Fine... Oh yes, I almost forget. If you come and visit the Matrix of Prescience, please view it from a distance. Outsiders are forbidden from approaching it without permission from the master diviner.

t_ch: 好了，没什么事的话以后再聊吧，我手里还有一大堆活儿没做完呢。
t_en: Okay, if there's nothing else, then let's talk again later. I've still got a mountain of work to get through.

t_ch: 云骑军在此执勤，请勿随意走动。
t_en: The Cloud Knights are on duty here. Please refrain from wandering around.

t_ch: …这不是随云骑军缉拿叛党的{NICKNAME}大人嘛！我听说过大人的威名，调任鳞渊境后，我还想着能不能有幸见上一面，没想到这就见到了。
t_en: ...If it isn't the mighty {NICKNAME} who rounded up the rebels with the Cloud Knights! I've long heard of your reputation. After being transferred to Scalegorge Waterscape, I wondered if I'd get the chance to meet you. And well, here we are!

t_ch: 可惜时机不巧，我现在正在公务执勤，您看这街道上人来人往，鳞渊境不知道还有多少暗流涌动，就藏在这风平浪静之下。
t_en: It's a shame that it's a bad time. I'm on duty right now. Just look at all these people going about their business peacefully. Who knows how many dark undercurrents still ripple beneath Scalegorge Waterscape, hidden just under the calm surface.

t_ch: 附近还有药王秘传残党？
t_en: Are there still remnants of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus nearby?

t_ch: 没错，药王秘传依然有残党混迹于此。只要建木和它的传说还在，求药的叛党就会根除不绝，我守在此处也是为了还这里百姓一份安全。
t_en: Yes. There're still a few Disciples of Sanctus Medicus lurking around here. So long as the Arbor and its legend exist, the Disciples will never truly be eradicated. I'm stationed here to give the few people remaining here a sense of security.

t_ch: 放心，都交给我来处理。
t_en: Don't worry. Let me handle everything.

t_ch: 大人的心意，我心领了。只要建木和它的传说还在，求药的叛党就会根除不绝，单凭你我是断然无法将他们处理干净的。
t_en: I appreciate your noblest of intentions, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. But as long as the Arbor and its legend exist, the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus will never truly be eradicated. There's no way you and I alone could take them all out.

t_ch: 所幸这一次神策府应对及时。如今他们成不了气候，我等只需要站在此处，他们便不敢随意造次。
t_en: Luckily, the Seat of Divine Foresight acted quickly this time. These days, the Disciples can't get up to anything. I just need to stand here, and they dare not make a move.

t_ch: 看见大人如此意气风发的样子，不禁想到自家的孩子…这话题不谈也罢，我还得站岗放哨呢，就不与大人闲谈了。还有别的事吗？
t_en: Seeing as you are in such high spirits, I can't help but think of my own child... But let's not go there. I've got my post to uphold, so I can't chitchat. Is there anything else on your mind?

t_ch: 能聊聊天吗？
t_en: Can we chat for a bit?

t_ch: 虽然我尚在执勤，但我实在没法拒绝您，适当聊几句还是没有问题的。
t_en: I may be on-duty, but who am I to refuse you? It should be fine to have a chat about something appropriate.

t_ch: 您是主动调任鳞渊境的？
t_en: Did you volunteer to be transferred to Scalegorge Waterscape?

t_ch: 大人心细，连这都记得。没错，我是主动请缨调往鳞渊境的。我儿…他在围剿药王秘传的战斗中牺牲了。
t_en: You have a fantastic memory, {F#ma'am}{M#sir}. That's right. I volunteered for this Scalegorge Waterscape assignment. My son... He lost his life in combat against the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus.

t_ch: 听到这个消息，我整宿整宿无法睡着。我想为他做些什么。他…年纪还轻，刚完成自己的初狩仪式，被授予了云骑的武器。
t_en: I couldn't sleep for many nights when I heard the news. I wanted to do something for him. He... He was still so young, and was just gifted a weapon of the Cloud Knights after he'd completed the ritual of his First Hunt.

t_ch: 我一直反对他和我一同入行伍，甚至为此闹得关系很糟。
t_en: I was always against him enlisting with me, and it took its toll on our relationship.

t_ch: 思来想去，还是我不够了解他。所以我申请调任鳞渊境，想在他曾经战斗的地方，为他再做点什么……
t_en: Thinking back over it, I still didn't understand him well enough. So I volunteered to transfer over to Scalegorge Waterscape. I want to be where he once fought, and do something for him...

t_ch: 虽然做什么都晚了。
t_en: Even though it's already too late...

t_ch: 药王秘传的残党棘手吗？
t_en: Are the remants of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus problematic?

t_ch: 蛰伏许久，为祸如此剧烈…当然棘手！
t_en: They lay dormant for so long, only to inflict such vicious damage... Of course they're problematic!

t_ch: 依我看来，如今这里往来的所有人，这些顶着面貌寻常、衣着普通的过客，都有可能是药王秘传未被清剿的残党。
t_en: In my opinion, all of these passers-by here with their ordinary faces and their ordinary clothes, any one of them could be a surviving remnant of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus.

t_ch: 但咱们无法一个个详加盘问，毕竟这里名义上是属于持明族共治的洞天。
t_en: But we can't just interrogate them one-by-one. After all, this delve is under Vidyadhara rule, at least on paper.

t_ch: 将军在此处派驻了云骑，一定是为了观望敌人的余势。
t_en: The general must have stationed us Cloud Knights here to keep an eye on the enemy's remnant forces.

t_ch: 听说有持明族不服管制。
t_en: Speaking of which, I've heard there are some Vidyadhara who refuse to comply?

t_ch: 确实如此，鳞渊境临近持明族的波月古海，那是他们浴月蜕生的地方，相当神圣…他们不希望有这么多持械士兵靠近，我也能理解。
t_en: There sure are. The Vidyadhara's Lunarescent Depths are near Scalegorge Waterscape. That's where they undergo their hatching rebirth, and it's a sacred place... They don't like having so many armed troops around, so I can see where they're coming from.

t_ch: 但是我也希望他们能理解云骑军。如今妖人一日没有清剿干净，这丹鼎司洞天就一日难得平静。有时候也挺羡慕他们的，哎……
t_en: But I also hope they can understand where the Cloud Knights are coming from, too. As long as the devils were not completely quelled, the Alchemy Commission delve will never see peace. Sometimes I even envy these Vidyadhara, you know...?

t_ch: 队长为何突发感慨呢？
t_en: Why did you get emotional all of a sudden, captain?

t_ch: 他们持明族只要能浴月蜕生，便能洗去一切烦恼重新来过，而我却要背负着痛苦继续生活下去。
t_en: Because the Vidyadhara can wash away all their troubles and get to start again with their hatching rebirth, but I have to keep on living with this burden of pain I bear.

t_ch: 前些日子我收到了军务厅的来信，让我接受测试，检查是否有陷入魔阴身的苗头。不过这又如何呢？我决心还是在这里站好最后一班岗。
t_en: A few days back, I got a letter from the military affairs office asking me to undergo a test to check if I have any signs of mara. But even if I do, so what? I'm determined to keep on standing here to the bitter end.

t_ch: 家家都有本难念的经。
t_en: Every family has their own difficulties.

t_ch: 您说的倒也没错，说不定持明族也在羡慕我呢。
t_en: You're right. Maybe the Vidyadhara envy me, too?

t_ch: 总之，这样的状态不会一直持续下去，我向你保证。
t_en: All in all, this situation won't last forever. I can promise you that.

t_ch: 我不便再打扰了。
t_en: I won't bother you any further.

t_ch: 大人请慢走。
t_en: Off you go. Take care.

t_ch: 我不便再打扰了。
t_en: I won't bother you any further.

t_ch: 大人请慢走。
t_en: Off you go. Take care.

t_en: I won't bother you any further.

t_ch: 大人请慢走。
t_en: Off you go. Take care.

t_ch: 危机总算解除了…
t_en: The crisis is finally over...

t_ch: 总算是解决了。犬子要是泉下有知，应该也会感到欣慰吧。
t_en: It is finally over. If my late son learned about it, I suppose he would feel relieved too.

t_ch: 他会为你骄傲的。
t_en: He would be proud of you.

t_ch: 我？哈哈哈，{NICKNAME}大人，您说笑了。
t_en: Me? Hahaha, you must be joking, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 我若当真是个值得骄傲的父亲，那他压根就不会……
t_en: If I had been a father that he was proud of, he wouldn't have...

t_ch: 但他，的的确确是一个值得我骄傲的儿子。
t_en: However, he was indeed a son that I was proud of.

t_ch: 欢迎光临，我们这里没有什么祖传秘方，只有常用的药和好用的药。
t_en: Welcome, welcome. We don't have any secret home recipes here. Just your everyday medicines and easy-to-use remedies.

t_ch: 如有需要，您可以随时喊我。当然，想要随便看看也无妨，请自便。
t_en: If you need me, just holler at me any time. Or just take a look yourself if you like. Whatever you prefer.

t_ch: 开门做生意，态度这么冷淡？
t_en: How do you get away with such a nonchalant attitude toward business?

t_ch: 做我们这行的——客人要是想买药，来之前心里多半就计划好要买什么，强行推销只会惹人烦，还是清净点好。
t_en: In our line of work, a patient who needs medicine will have already planned out what they need to buy. People find a hard sell annoying, so some peace and quiet is probably better.

t_ch: 今时今日这个态度可不行。
t_en: That attitude is completely unacceptable in this day and age.

t_ch: 我天性如此，并非针对您，客官不必见怪。
t_en: It's just the way I am. Please don't take any offense. It's nothing personal.

t_ch: 我这人喜清净，何况我开的还是药店，药店嘛，还是清净点好。太热闹了，也未必是什么好事…长命钱呐，还得长命挣。
t_en: I'm a person who likes peace and quiet. Not to mention that I run a pharmacy. That's right, a pharmacy. So peace and quiet is best. If things are too bustling around here, it's not necessarily a good thing... Long-life species earn their salaries the long way, y'know.

t_ch: 好了，要是没事，容我先坐下喝口茶——
t_en: Alright. If it's okay with you, then I'll just take a seat and have a sip of tea...

t_ch: 我想问…
t_en: I'd like to ask...

t_ch: 你好像在一直盯着外面看？
t_en: Why do you always seem to be peering outside?

t_ch: 我的店铺开在这儿，外面有什么风吹草动的，我看两眼很正常。
t_en: This is my shop, so it's normal for me to keep both eyes peeled for any troubles that might arise.

t_ch: 像我这种遵纪守法的小商人，要是遇见什么可疑的人和事了，我定然会及时上报到神策府去。
t_en: As a law-abiding small businessman, any suspicious character I come across is going to get reported to the Seat of Divine Foresight immediately.

t_ch: 听你口气，好像和神策府很熟？
t_en: Judging by your tone, you sound pretty familiar with the Seat of Divine Foresight?

t_ch: 咳——我之前和云骑军举报过几次化外民的可疑行为，仅此而已，你别瞎想。
t_en: *Ahem* I've reported a few suspicious Outworlders to the Cloud Knights before, that's all. Don't overthink it.

t_ch: 咱们罗浮的居民，还是得和云骑通力合作，这样才能保持现有的安宁嘛。这点自觉我还是有的。
t_en: Here on the Luofu, we still have to cooperate fully with the Cloud Knights to keep the peace around here. I'm self-aware enough to get that.

t_ch: 外面发生了什么事吗？
t_en: What happened outside?

t_ch: 那边的持明好像在嚷嚷什么，我也不清楚。
t_en: There're some Vidyadhara yelling about something over there. I don't really know what.

t_ch: 您要是听到些什么风声，倒是可以跟我说说，反正我闲着也是闲着嘛。
t_en: Fill me in if you hear any rumors out there. In any case, I'm just going to be stuck here.

t_ch: 能给我推荐点必备良药吗？
t_en: Can you recommend some good essential medicines for me?

t_ch: 我看您面色红润身手矫健，想来也没什么毛病，还是算了吧。
t_en: You look full of life and vigor. There's nothing wrong with you, so don't worry about it.

t_ch: 你这个态度不像卖药的……
t_en: You don't seem to care much about making sales at all...

t_ch: 连现成的生意都不做？
t_en: You don't even want to close this easy deal right in front of you?
